tree.name: 0) Store grid-scale renewable energy
tree.description: Develop novel methods to store grid-scale renewable energy that do not rely on rare-earth lithium-ion batteries, targeting cost, scalability, and environmental sustainability

achievers.name: Local Communities
achievers.description: These are the residents, businesses, and local governments situated in geographic proximity to where grid-scale energy storage facilities would be developed and operated. Their quality of life, local environment, and economic well-being can be directly affected, making their buy-in crucial for project siting, permitting, and long-term social license.

achievers.name: Preserved Local Environmental Quality
achievers.description: This goal seeks to ensure that the development and operation of novel grid-scale energy storage facilities do not adversely impact the natural environment surrounding local communities. It focuses on maintaining the health of local ecosystems, including air, water, soil, and biodiversity, while safeguarding the aesthetic value of the area. Achieving this ensures a high quality of life for residents and protects local natural resources.

achievers.name: Subterranean & Brownfield Storage Integration
achievers.description: Implement novel grid-scale energy storage solutions primarily within subterranean spaces or strategically integrated industrial brownfield locations. This involves utilizing technologies such as advanced underground compressed air energy storage (UCAES) or gravity-based systems leveraging disused mines, caverns, or existing industrial infrastructure. This approach drastically reduces the surface land footprint and visual impact, preserving natural landscapes, biodiversity, and aesthetic value for local communities. It also inherently minimizes noise, dust, and light pollution by keeping most operational infrastructure out of sight, safeguarding local environmental quality.

achievers.name: Ecological Symbiosis Storage Systems
achievers.description: {'What is the solution?': 'Develop grid-scale energy storage systems that are designed to be ecologically symbiotic, actively enhancing local natural environments rather than merely mitigating impact. This involves integrating storage infrastructure with restored or created ecosystems, such as advanced thermal storage co-located with engineered wetlands or nature-based solutions utilizing abundant, non-toxic materials.', 'Why is it a good solution?': 'This approach transforms energy storage facilities into ecological assets, providing benefits like biodiversity enhancement, water filtration, and carbon sequestration alongside energy services. It ensures the use of environmentally benign and often locally sourced materials, fostering a net positive environmental impact and greater community acceptance.'}

achievers.name: Advanced Circularity Storage Systems
achievers.description: {'What is the solution?': 'Develop and deploy grid-scale energy storage systems engineered as fully closed-loop, self-sustaining units that achieve complete material circularity. This involves designing facilities to minimize external resource intake, prevent any operational discharge or emissions, and feature 100% on-site recycling or reprocessing of all components and storage media at end-of-life.', 'Why is it a good solution?': 'This approach virtually eliminates environmental impact by containing all materials and processes within the facility, preventing pollution of local air, water, and soil. It ensures responsible resource management, drastically reduces waste generation, and removes the risk of long-term contamination, thereby preserving the pristine quality of surrounding natural environments for local communities.'}

achievers.name: Visually Integrated Distributed Storage
achievers.description: {'What is the solution?': 'Develop modular, distributed grid-scale energy storage units designed for seamless visual and spatial integration into diverse local environments. This involves camouflaging structures to mimic natural landforms, utilizing natural or non-reflective materials, and incorporating bio-inspired architecture to minimize aesthetic disruption and physical footprint.', 'Why is it a good solution?': 'This approach prevents visual pollution and maintains the aesthetic value of local landscapes by making storage facilities blend in or appear as natural features. By distributing smaller units, it reduces the impact of a single large facility, preserving local ecological balance and community quality of life without demanding large dedicated land parcels.'}
